description: Repo-local dogfood examples for Lane 1 finding alignment. Raw findings are supporting evidence; canonical items are countable; actionable canonical gaps are user work.
limits: no gate or public score changes
no generated tests, provider calls, source edits, or mutation execution

cases.raw_finding_summary: A declaration raw finding and assigned literal raw finding group into one presentation_text canonical item.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: the receipt pinned raw-to-canonical counts only; after the refresh: it also pins canonical_gap_id, raw summary, repair route, and verify route.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a help-output snapshot assertion for the changed presentation text.
must_not_claim: Do not count the declaration and literal as two user actions.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
cases.reason: A supported user-visible help label without an observer should become one actionable canonical output-observer repair.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A declaration raw finding and assigned literal raw finding group into one already-observed presentation_text item.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: the receipt pinned the no-action outcome without an explicit route; after the refresh: it records the existing-output-observer route and canonical_gap_id.
cases.recommended_repair: No new RIPR action; the existing golden observer covers the rendered output.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable gap when a supported output observer exists.
Do not hide the raw findings.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
cases.reason: A supported rendered-output observer should keep the canonical item visible as already observed, not user repair work.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A policy metadata declaration and literal group into one internal-only config_or_policy_constant item.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: internal policy metadata was counted as one no-action item; after the refresh: the receipt also carries the no-action route and canonical_gap_id.
cases.recommended_repair: No user test action; the constant is internal policy metadata in fixture-backed scope.
must_not_claim: Do not create user test debt from internal policy metadata alone.
Do not hide the raw declaration and literal findings.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
cases.reason: Internal policy metadata should align into one canonical no-action item instead of a user-facing gap.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A rendered policy-label declaration and literal group into one actionable config_or_policy_constant item.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: the rendered-label example lacked explicit route fields; after the refresh: it pins the output-observer route and canonical_gap_id.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a report-render, config-output, snapshot, or golden observer for the rendered policy label.
must_not_claim: Do not count declaration and literal findings as separate user actions.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
cases.reason: A rendered config or policy label with no supported observer should become one actionable output-observer item.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A behavior selector declaration and literal group into one actionable behavior-discriminator item.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: behavior-selector receipts named the target test type only; after the refresh: they pin the behavior-discriminator route and canonical_gap_id.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a validation or routing behavior discriminator for the changed selector.
must_not_claim: Do not treat behavior selectors as output labels.
Do not count declaration and literal findings as separate user actions.
Do not accept lexical-only tests as behavior discriminators.
Do not recommend mutation testing before behavior-discriminator work.
cases.reason: A supported validation or routing selector without a discriminator should become one actionable canonical behavior-discriminator repair.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A behavior selector declaration and literal group into one already-observed validation behavior item.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: observed behavior-selector receipts lacked explicit no-action route fields; after the refresh: the existing validation observer route is recorded.
cases.recommended_repair: No new RIPR action; the existing validation behavior test covers the selector.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable behavior-discriminator gap when a supported behavior observer exists.
Do not hide the raw selector findings.
Do not infer observation from lexical mentions alone.
cases.reason: A supported validation or routing discriminator should keep the selector visible as already observed, not user repair work.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A config constant declaration and literal group into one named static limitation item because the flow is untraceable.
cases.before_after_context: Before the dogfood refresh: unknown flow was named as a limitation; after the refresh: the limitation route, raw summary, and canonical_gap_id are explicitly receipted.
cases.recommended_repair: Trace the config constant to a supported output, schema, validation, or behavior sink before claiming user test debt.
must_not_claim: Do not treat generic static_unknown as user test debt.
Do not hide the raw findings.
Do not recommend mutation testing before naming the static limitation.
cases.reason: Untraceable config flow should remain a named analyzer limitation with a repair route, not a user-facing test gap.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A config label declaration and literal group into one named opaque lookup limitation item.
cases.before_after_context: Before #1144: every opaque lookup shape stayed limited; after #1144: this generic opaque lookup guard still remains a limitation while the fixture-backed report lookup has its own actionable receipt.
cases.recommended_repair: Inspect the opaque config lookup path or add fixture-backed analyzer support before claiming output-observer debt.
must_not_claim: Do not treat opaque lookup static_unknown as user test debt.
Do not infer user-visible config output through an opaque helper.
Do not hide the raw declaration and literal findings.
cases.reason: An opaque config lookup should stay a named static limitation until the analyzer can trace the lookup shape.

cases.raw_finding_summary: Declaration, literal, and fixture-backed lookup sink raw findings group into one opaque report lookup canonical item.
cases.before_after_context: Before #1144: opaque_config_lookup remained a named static limitation; after #1144: fixture-backed OPAQUE_REPORT_LABEL lookup is actionable while generic opaque lookup remains limited.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a report-render or golden-output test for OPAQUE_REPORT_LABEL.
must_not_claim: Do not infer user-visible output from helper names alone.
Do not apply this route to registry, macro-generated, dynamic-dispatch, or cross-file lookup shapes.
Do not treat the actionable gap as runtime mutation proof.
cases.reason: Fixture-backed opaque report lookups can become actionable output-observer gaps without widening generic lookup support.

cases.raw_finding_summary: One weakly_gripped predicate_boundary raw finding for `&function.id == owner` groups into one actionable canonical gap.
cases.before_after_context: Before #1146: missing verify command sentinels could satisfy dogfood shape; after #1146 and the current sampled audit: 61 actionable predicate_boundary items report concrete verify commands and zero canonical items without verify commands.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or strengthen `assert_eq!(resolve_owner_function(/* boundary input where &function.id == owner */), /* expected */)` for the owner equality boundary.
must_not_claim: Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
Do not treat raw findings as independent user work.
Do not claim mutation execution or runtime proof from this packet.
cases.reason: A sampled actionable predicate_boundary packet now carries a concrete repair route and verify command, preserving the raw-finding to canonical-item boundary.
